住吉神社_(加西市)
[[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]]

{{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |Location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |Image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * **Middle Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **East Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **West Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **Worship Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * **Heiden** (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * **White Beard Shrine** (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * **Two Inari Shrines** * **Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)** (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * **Treasury Hall** * **Chokushi Mound** - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * **Four Structures** (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * **White Beard Shrine** (with associated ridge plaque) * **Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)** (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * **Heiden** * **Awashima Shrine** ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==Location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]]

{{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |Location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |Image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * **Middle Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **East Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **West Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **Worship Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * **Heiden** (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * **White Beard Shrine** (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * **Two Inari Shrines** * **Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)** (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * **Treasury Hall** * **Chokushi Mound** - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * **Four Structures** (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * **White Beard Shrine** (with associated ridge plaque) * **Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)** (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * **Heiden** * **Awashima Shrine** ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==Location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:new pages]][[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]]

{{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |Location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |Image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * **Middle Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **East Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **West Main Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * **Worship Hall** (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * **Heiden** (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * **White Beard Shrine** (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * **Two Inari Shrines** * **Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)** (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * **Treasury Hall** * **Chokushi Mound** - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * **Four Structures** (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * **White Beard Shrine** (with associated ridge plaque) * **Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)** (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * **Heiden** * **Awashima Shrine** ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==Location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]]

{{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |Location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |Image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==Location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==Location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |Location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |Image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==Location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |Location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |Image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==Location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō {{Shrine |Name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |Deities={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |OtherNames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==Deities== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Autumn Festival of Banshū|ja|播州の秋祭り}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sakami Temple|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]]
