revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 境内" to "== Shrine facilities"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=住吉神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[兵庫県]][[加西市]][[北条町北条]]1318 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 7.1 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 42.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=酒見神
[[住吉三神|住吉四神]]（底筒男命、中筒男命、表筒男命、[[神功皇后]]）
[[大歳神]]
[[八幡神]] |創建=[[養老]]元年（[[717]]） | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=旧[[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival=春季例大祭【北条節句祭り】（4月第1土・日曜日） |神事= |本殿=住吉造 |別名=酒見大明神、住吉酒見社、住吉酒見神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
本殿（国の[[重要文化財]]） |地図=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|拝殿（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[加西市]]にある[[神社]]。'''酒見大明神'''、'''住吉酒見社'''、'''住吉酒見神社'''とも。[[播磨国]]三の宮、旧[[式内社]]、旧[[近代社格制度| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type]]は[[県社]]。 == History == 黒駒村で創建され、[[養老]]元年（[[717]]）に現在地に遷し奉られた。[[大阪]]の[[住吉大社]]らに伝わる『[[住吉大社神代記]]』〔[[天平]]3年（[[731]]）〕に記された[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]の宮九箇処の一社である。古くは酒見大明神や酒見社と呼ばれており、[[明治時代]]に[[県社]]に列格された際に現在の社名に改められた。天平17年（[[745]]）には[[行基]]によって東隣に別当寺である[[酒見寺]]が建立された。 社殿は[[平治]]元年（[[1159]]）の[[Heiji rebellion]]、[[建永]]2年（[[1207]]）、[[天正]]年間（[[1573]] - [[1592]]）の兵火など、幾度もの焼失と再建を経て荒廃していたところ、[[江戸時代]]初期に[[姫路城]]主の[[池田輝政]]により復興された。輝政によって年々三十石の寄進を受けている。その後[[姫路藩]]主となった本多美濃守によって社領は六十石となった。 現在の本社三殿（重要文化財）は[[嘉永]]4年（[[1851]]）に再建されたものである。 [[1874]]（[[明治]]7年）に郷社、[[1881]]（明治14年）には県社に列せられる。[[1909]]（明治42年）に大歳神社と八幡神社を合祀している。 == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * 主祭神 - 酒見神、[[住吉三神|住吉四神]]（底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命・[[神功皇后]]）、[[大歳神]]、[[八幡神]] == Shrine facilities == * 中本殿（国の重要文化財） - [[嘉永]]4年（[[1851]]）再建。[[1916]]（[[大正]]5年）改築。 * 東本殿（国の重要文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。1916年（大正5年）改築。 * 西本殿（国の重要文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。1916年（大正5年）改築。 * 幣殿（加西市指定有形文化財） - 1916年（大正5年）再建。 * 拝殿（国の重要文化財） - [[Bunka]]5年（[[1808]]）再建。 * 玉垣（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 1900（明治33年）築。 * [[粟島神社]]（加西市指定有形文化財） - [[明治]]時代。 * [[白鬚神社]]（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。 * [[稲荷社]] - 稲荷社は2基ある。 * 稲荷社 * 神社会館 - 社務所。 * 勅使塚 - 境内中央にあり、現在は「[[闘鶏|鶏合わせ]]」や「龍王舞」など[[神事]]の場として使われている。 * 手水舎（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 文化5年（1808年）再建。 == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 住吉神社 4棟（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）12月12日指定令和4年12月12日文部科学省告示第155号。。 ''' 東本殿（附 棟札1枚） ''' 中本殿（附 厨子1基） ''' 西本殿（附 棟札1枚） ''' 拝殿 === 兵庫県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ''' 住吉神社 2棟（建造物） - [[2017]]（[[平成]]29年）3月14日、中本殿・東本殿・西本殿・拝殿・白鬚神社・手水舎の6棟の指定{{PDFlink|[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/kk32/koho/documents/290314t.pdf 平成29年3月14日兵庫県公報]}}より兵庫県教育委員会告示第4号（リンクは兵庫県ホームページ）。。2022年（令和4年）12月12日、中本殿・東本殿・西本殿・拝殿の4棟の国の重要文化財指定（県指定解除）。 '''* 白鬚神社（附：棟札1枚） '''* 手水舎（附：棟札2枚） * 無形民俗文化財 ''' 住吉神社竜王の舞（住吉神社鶏合せ龍王舞保存会） === 加西市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ''' 幣殿 ''' 粟島神社 * 無形民俗文化財 ''' 住吉神社鶏合せ（住吉神社鶏合せ龍王舞保存会） なお、以下の建造物6件は、[[2005]]（平成17年）11月10日付けで国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されていたが、兵庫県及び加西市の有形文化財に指定されたことにともない、当該登録は抹消されている。 * 中本殿、東本殿、西本殿、拝殿、玉垣 - 県指定にともない、2017年3月14日付けで登録抹消{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/yukei_kenzobutsu/massho/todofuken.html |title=都道府県指定による登録抹消|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-07-29}} * 幣殿 - 市指定にともない2016年5月23日付けで登録抹消{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/yukei_kenzobutsu/massho/shichoson.html |title=市町村指定による登録抹消|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-07-29}} == 春季例大祭 (北条節句祭り) == 毎年4月の第1土曜日から翌日に行われる春季例大祭は「北条節句祭り」と呼ばれ播州三大祭の1つに数えられる。 [[播州の秋祭り]]と同様の'''反り屋根布団屋台'''（[[太鼓台]]）が登場する。北条では屋台の事を別称「化粧屋台」と呼ぶ事があり、東郷から8台（'''本町・横尾・東高室・古坂・西高室・南町・栗田・御旅町'''）、西郷から7台（'''谷・小谷・笠屋・西上野・市村・宮前・黒駒'''）の計15台の屋台が運行される。 屋台関係者は、着物に長襦袢という播州地方では珍しい格好で祭礼に参加する。基本的には'''紺'''か'''黒'''の着物で男物の長襦袢を着るが、一部の地区では灰色や茶色、女物の着物を仕立て直しして長襦袢として着る人もいる。ちなみに[[滋賀県]][[愛荘町]]で行われる[[堅井之大宮]](軽野神社)春季大祭は北条節句祭り同様、着物に長襦袢という格好で祭礼が行われている。 また、各地区では鉢巻と襷で色分けされている。 * '''東郷''' ''' '''本町'''〔ピンク色〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''横尾(榮壽)'''〔黄色〕※鶏合わせ奉仕地区 ''' '''東高室'''〔赤色〕 ''' '''古坂(勇寳)'''〔黄緑色〕 ''' '''西高室'''〔白色〕 ''' '''南町(旭光)'''〔水色(新橋色)〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''栗田(松之尾)'''〔薄黄色〕※龍王舞奉仕地区 ''' '''御旅町(住之江)'''〔濃い青色〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加 ''' '''東南'''〔水色〕※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は参加。 ''' '''福吉'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''駅前'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''栄町　'''※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 * '''西郷''' ''' '''谷(寳光)'''〔臙脂色〕※龍王舞(締太鼓)奉仕地区 ''' '''小谷(龍虎)'''〔薄水色(蒼色)〕※龍王舞(舞)奉仕地区 ''' '''笠屋(明寳)'''〔橙色〕※神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''西上野'''〔白色〕※鶏合わせ奉仕地区 ''' '''市村(卯の日講)'''〔水色〕※龍王舞(獅子頭)奉仕地区 ''' '''宮前'''〔紫色〕※神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''黒駒'''〔薄桃色〕 ''' '''御幸町'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''江ノ木'''　※子供神輿での奉納。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''住屋町　'''※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 == 所在地 == 兵庫県加西市北条町北条1318 == 交通アクセス == * [[北条鉄道北条線]] [[北条町駅]] 徒歩15分 == 周辺情報 == * [[酒見寺]]（隣接） * [[羅漢寺 (加西市)]] - 元々は酒見寺の末寺。 * [[神明神社]] - 笠屋屋台蔵と隣接。 * [[大年神社]] - 住吉神社御旅所。境内の中に南町屋台蔵がある。 * [[イオンモール加西北条]] *[[加西市立北条小学校]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm |title=Web site播州北条節句祭 |date=20160304132957}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|すみよししんしや かさいし}} [[Category:住吉神社]] [[category:播磨国|社3]] [[Category:加西市の歴史]] [[Category:加西市の建築物]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a [[Category:already a redirect]] and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |名称=住吉神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[兵庫県]][[加西市]][[北条町北条]]1318 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 7.1 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 42.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=酒見神
[[住吉三神|住吉四神]]（底筒男命、中筒男命、表筒男命、[[神功皇后]]）
[[大歳神]]
[[八幡神]] |創建=[[養老]]元年（[[717]]） | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=旧[[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival=春季例大祭【北条節句祭り】（4月第1土・日曜日） |神事= |本殿=住吉造 |別名=酒見大明神、住吉酒見社、住吉酒見神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
本殿（国の[[重要文化財]]） |地図=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|拝殿（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[加西市]]にある[[神社]]。'''酒見大明神'''、'''住吉酒見社'''、'''住吉酒見神社'''とも。[[播磨国]]三の宮、旧[[式内社]]、旧[[近代社格制度| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type]]は[[県社]]。 == History == 黒駒村で創建され、[[養老]]元年（[[717]]）に現在地に遷し奉られた。[[大阪]]の[[住吉大社]]らに伝わる『[[住吉大社神代記]]』〔[[天平]]3年（[[731]]）〕に記された[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]の宮九箇処の一社である。古くは酒見大明神や酒見社と呼ばれており、[[明治時代]]に[[県社]]に列格された際に現在の社名に改められた。天平17年（[[745]]）には[[行基]]によって東隣に別当寺である[[酒見寺]]が建立された。 社殿は[[平治]]元年（[[1159]]）の[[Heiji rebellion]]、[[建永]]2年（[[1207]]）、[[天正]]年間（[[1573]] - [[1592]]）の兵火など、幾度もの焼失と再建を経て荒廃していたところ、[[江戸時代]]初期に[[姫路城]]主の[[池田輝政]]により復興された。輝政によって年々三十石の寄進を受けている。その後[[姫路藩]]主となった本多美濃守によって社領は六十石となった。 現在の本社三殿（重要文化財）は[[嘉永]]4年（[[1851]]）に再建されたものである。 [[1874]]（[[明治]]7年）に郷社、[[1881]]（明治14年）には県社に列せられる。[[1909]]（明治42年）に大歳神社と八幡神社を合祀している。 == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * 主祭神 - 酒見神、[[住吉三神|住吉四神]]（底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命・[[神功皇后]]）、[[大歳神]]、[[八幡神]] == Shrine facilities == * 中本殿（国の重要文化財） - [[嘉永]]4年（[[1851]]）再建。[[1916]]（[[大正]]5年）改築。 * 東本殿（国の重要文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。1916年（大正5年）改築。 * 西本殿（国の重要文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。1916年（大正5年）改築。 * 幣殿（加西市指定有形文化財） - 1916年（大正5年）再建。 * 拝殿（国の重要文化財） - [[Bunka]]5年（[[1808]]）再建。 * 玉垣（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 1900（明治33年）築。 * [[粟島神社]]（加西市指定有形文化財） - [[明治]]時代。 * [[白鬚神社]]（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。 * [[稲荷社]] - 県指定にともない、2017年3月14日付けで登録抹消{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/yukei_kenzobutsu/massho/todofuken.html |title=都道府県指定による登録抹消|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-07-29}} * 幣殿 - 市指定にともない2016年5月23日付けで登録抹消{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/yukei_kenzobutsu/massho/shichoson.html |title=市町村指定による登録抹消|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-07-29}} == 春季例大祭 (北条節句祭り) == 毎年4月の第1土曜日から翌日に行われる春季例大祭は「北条節句祭り」と呼ばれ播州三大祭の1つに数えられる。 [[播州の秋祭り]]と同様の'''反り屋根布団屋台'''（[[太鼓台]]）が登場する。北条では屋台の事を別称「化粧屋台」と呼ぶ事があり、東郷から8台（'''本町・横尾・東高室・古坂・西高室・南町・栗田・御旅町'''）、西郷から7台（'''谷・小谷・笠屋・西上野・市村・宮前・黒駒'''）の計15台の屋台が運行される。 屋台関係者は、着物に長襦袢という播州地方では珍しい格好で祭礼に参加する。基本的には'''紺'''か'''黒'''の着物で男物の長襦袢を着るが、一部の地区では灰色や茶色、女物の着物を仕立て直しして長襦袢として着る人もいる。ちなみに[[滋賀県]][[愛荘町]]で行われる[[堅井之大宮]](軽野神社)春季大祭は北条節句祭り同様、着物に長襦袢という格好で祭礼が行われている。 また、各地区では鉢巻と襷で色分けされている。 * '''東郷''' ''' '''本町'''〔ピンク色〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''横尾(榮壽)'''〔黄色〕※鶏合わせ奉仕地区 ''' '''東高室'''〔赤色〕 ''' '''古坂(勇寳)'''〔黄緑色〕 ''' '''西高室'''〔白色〕 ''' '''南町(旭光)'''〔水色(新橋色)〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''栗田(松之尾)'''〔薄黄色〕※龍王舞奉仕地区 ''' '''御旅町(住之江)'''〔濃い青色〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加 ''' '''東南'''〔水色〕※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は参加。 ''' '''福吉'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''駅前'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''栄町　'''※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 * '''西郷''' ''' '''谷(寳光)'''〔臙脂色〕※龍王舞(締太鼓)奉仕地区 ''' '''小谷(龍虎)'''〔薄水色(蒼色)〕※龍王舞(舞)奉仕地区 ''' '''笠屋(明寳)'''〔橙色〕※神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''西上野'''〔白色〕※鶏合わせ奉仕地区 ''' '''市村(卯の日講)'''〔水色〕※龍王舞(獅子頭)奉仕地区 ''' '''宮前'''〔紫色〕※神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''黒駒'''〔薄桃色〕 ''' '''御幸町'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''江ノ木'''　※子供神輿での奉納。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''住屋町　'''※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 == 所在地 == 兵庫県加西市北条町北条1318 == 交通アクセス == * [[北条鉄道北条線]] [[北条町駅]] 徒歩15分 == 周辺情報 == * [[酒見寺]]（隣接） * [[羅漢寺 (加西市)]] - 元々は酒見寺の末寺。 * [[神明神社]] - 笠屋屋台蔵と隣接。 * [[大年神社]] - 住吉神社御旅所。境内の中に南町屋台蔵がある。 * [[イオンモール加西北条]] *[[加西市立北条小学校]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm |title=Web site播州北条節句祭 |date=20160304132957}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|すみよししんしや かさいし}} [[Category:住吉神社]] [[category:播磨国|社3]] [[Category:加西市の歴史]] [[Category:加西市の建築物]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a [[Category:already a redirect]] and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages to be overwritten]]

{{神社 |名称=住吉神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[兵庫県]][[加西市]][[北条町北条]]1318 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 56 | 緯度秒 = 7.1 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 42.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity=酒見神
[[住吉三神|住吉四神]]（底筒男命、中筒男命、表筒男命、[[神功皇后]]）
[[大歳神]]
[[八幡神]] |創建=[[養老]]元年（[[717]]） | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type=旧[[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival=春季例大祭【北条節句祭り】（4月第1土・日曜日） |神事= |本殿=住吉造 |別名=酒見大明神、住吉酒見社、住吉酒見神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
本殿（国の[[重要文化財]]） |地図=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|拝殿（国の[[重要文化財]]）]] '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[加西市]]にある[[神社]]。'''酒見大明神'''、'''住吉酒見社'''、'''住吉酒見神社'''とも。[[播磨国]]三の宮、旧[[式内社]]、旧[[近代社格制度| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type]]は[[県社]]。 == History == 黒駒村で創建され、[[養老]]元年（[[717]]）に現在地に遷し奉られた。[[大阪]]の[[住吉大社]]らに伝わる『[[住吉大社神代記]]』〔[[天平]]3年（[[731]]）〕に記された[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]の宮九箇処の一社である。古くは酒見大明神や酒見社と呼ばれており、[[明治時代]]に[[県社]]に列格された際に現在の社名に改められた。天平17年（[[745]]）には[[行基]]によって東隣に別当寺である[[酒見寺]]が建立された。 社殿は[[平治]]元年（[[1159]]）の[[Heiji rebellion]]、[[建永]]2年（[[1207]]）、[[天正]]年間（[[1573]] - [[1592]]）の兵火など、幾度もの焼失と再建を経て荒廃していたところ、[[江戸時代]]初期に[[姫路城]]主の[[池田輝政]]により復興された。輝政によって年々三十石の寄進を受けている。その後[[姫路藩]]主となった本多美濃守によって社領は六十石となった。 現在の本社三殿（重要文化財）は[[嘉永]]4年（[[1851]]）に再建されたものである。 [[1874]]（[[明治]]7年）に郷社、[[1881]]（明治14年）には県社に列せられる。[[1909]]（明治42年）に大歳神社と八幡神社を合祀している。 == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * 主祭神 - 酒見神、[[住吉三神|住吉四神]]（底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命・[[神功皇后]]）、[[大歳神]]、[[八幡神]] == Shrine facilities == * 中本殿（国の重要文化財） - [[嘉永]]4年（[[1851]]）再建。[[1916]]（[[大正]]5年）改築。 * 東本殿（国の重要文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。1916年（大正5年）改築。 * 西本殿（国の重要文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。1916年（大正5年）改築。 * 幣殿（加西市指定有形文化財） - 1916年（大正5年）再建。 * 拝殿（国の重要文化財） - [[Bunka]]5年（[[1808]]）再建。 * 玉垣（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 1900（明治33年）築。 * [[粟島神社]]（加西市指定有形文化財） - [[明治]]時代。 * [[白鬚神社]]（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 嘉永4年（1851年）再建。 * [[稲荷社]] - 稲荷社は2基ある。 * 稲荷社 * 神社会館 - 社務所。 * 勅使塚 - 境内中央にあり、現在は「[[闘鶏|鶏合わせ]]」や「龍王舞」など[[神事]]の場として使われている。 * 手水舎（兵庫県指定有形文化財） - 文化5年（1808年）再建。 == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 住吉神社 4棟（建造物） - 2022年（令和4年）12月12日指定令和4年12月12日文部科学省告示第155号。。 ''' 東本殿（附 棟札1枚） ''' 中本殿（附 厨子1基） ''' 西本殿（附 棟札1枚） ''' 拝殿 === 兵庫県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ''' 住吉神社 2棟（建造物） - [[2017]]（[[平成]]29年）3月14日、中本殿・東本殿・西本殿・拝殿・白鬚神社・手水舎の6棟の指定{{PDFlink|[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/kk32/koho/documents/290314t.pdf 平成29年3月14日兵庫県公報]}}より兵庫県教育委員会告示第4号（リンクは兵庫県ホームページ）。。2022年（令和4年）12月12日、中本殿・東本殿・西本殿・拝殿の4棟の国の重要文化財指定（県指定解除）。 '''* 白鬚神社（附：棟札1枚） '''* 手水舎（附：棟札2枚） * 無形民俗文化財 ''' 住吉神社竜王の舞（住吉神社鶏合せ龍王舞保存会） === 加西市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ''' 幣殿 ''' 粟島神社 * 無形民俗文化財 ''' 住吉神社鶏合せ（住吉神社鶏合せ龍王舞保存会） なお、以下の建造物6件は、[[2005]]（平成17年）11月10日付けで国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されていたが、兵庫県及び加西市の有形文化財に指定されたことにともない、当該登録は抹消されている。 * 中本殿、東本殿、西本殿、拝殿、玉垣 - 県指定にともない、2017年3月14日付けで登録抹消{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/yukei_kenzobutsu/massho/todofuken.html |title=都道府県指定による登録抹消|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-07-29}} * 幣殿 - 市指定にともない2016年5月23日付けで登録抹消{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/yukei_kenzobutsu/massho/shichoson.html |title=市町村指定による登録抹消|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-07-29}} == 春季例大祭 (北条節句祭り) == 毎年4月の第1土曜日から翌日に行われる春季例大祭は「北条節句祭り」と呼ばれ播州三大祭の1つに数えられる。 [[播州の秋祭り]]と同様の'''反り屋根布団屋台'''（[[太鼓台]]）が登場する。北条では屋台の事を別称「化粧屋台」と呼ぶ事があり、東郷から8台（'''本町・横尾・東高室・古坂・西高室・南町・栗田・御旅町'''）、西郷から7台（'''谷・小谷・笠屋・西上野・市村・宮前・黒駒'''）の計15台の屋台が運行される。 屋台関係者は、着物に長襦袢という播州地方では珍しい格好で祭礼に参加する。基本的には'''紺'''か'''黒'''の着物で男物の長襦袢を着るが、一部の地区では灰色や茶色、女物の着物を仕立て直しして長襦袢として着る人もいる。ちなみに[[滋賀県]][[愛荘町]]で行われる[[堅井之大宮]](軽野神社)春季大祭は北条節句祭り同様、着物に長襦袢という格好で祭礼が行われている。 また、各地区では鉢巻と襷で色分けされている。 * '''東郷''' ''' '''本町'''〔ピンク色〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''横尾(榮壽)'''〔黄色〕※鶏合わせ奉仕地区 ''' '''東高室'''〔赤色〕 ''' '''古坂(勇寳)'''〔黄緑色〕 ''' '''西高室'''〔白色〕 ''' '''南町(旭光)'''〔水色(新橋色)〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''栗田(松之尾)'''〔薄黄色〕※龍王舞奉仕地区 ''' '''御旅町(住之江)'''〔濃い青色〕※神輿奉仕は北条東で参加 ''' '''東南'''〔水色〕※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は参加。 ''' '''福吉'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''駅前'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 ''' '''栄町　'''※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条東で参加。 * '''西郷''' ''' '''谷(寳光)'''〔臙脂色〕※龍王舞(締太鼓)奉仕地区 ''' '''小谷(龍虎)'''〔薄水色(蒼色)〕※龍王舞(舞)奉仕地区 ''' '''笠屋(明寳)'''〔橙色〕※神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''西上野'''〔白色〕※鶏合わせ奉仕地区 ''' '''市村(卯の日講)'''〔水色〕※龍王舞(獅子頭)奉仕地区 ''' '''宮前'''〔紫色〕※神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''黒駒'''〔薄桃色〕 ''' '''御幸町'''　※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''江ノ木'''　※子供神輿での奉納。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 ''' '''住屋町　'''※屋台奉納は無し。神輿奉仕は北条西で参加。 == 所在地 == 兵庫県加西市北条町北条1318 == 交通アクセス == * [[北条鉄道北条線]] [[北条町駅]] 徒歩15分 == 周辺情報 == * [[酒見寺]]（隣接） * [[羅漢寺 (加西市)]] - 元々は酒見寺の末寺。 * [[神明神社]] - 笠屋屋台蔵と隣接。 * [[大年神社]] - 住吉神社御旅所。境内の中に南町屋台蔵がある。 * [[イオンモール加西北条]] *[[加西市立北条小学校]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm |title=Web site播州北条節句祭 |date=20160304132957}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|すみよししんしや かさいし}} [[Category:住吉神社]] [[category:播磨国|社3]] [[Category:加西市の歴史]] [[Category:加西市の建築物]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a [[Category:already a redirect]] and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:住吉神社 (加西市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages to be overwritten]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) - East Main Hall - Middle Main Hall - West Main Hall - Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=draft}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=draft}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱|12=draft}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永|12=draft}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=draft}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政|12=draft}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩|12=draft}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏|12=draft}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=draft}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=draft}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅|12=draft}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=draft}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|12=draft}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=draft}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=draft}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=draft}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱|12=draft}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永|12=draft}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=draft}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政|12=draft}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩|12=draft}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏|12=draft}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=draft}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=draft}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅|12=draft}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=draft}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|12=draft}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=draft}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=draft}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=draft}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱|12=draft}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永|12=draft}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=draft}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政|12=draft}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩|12=draft}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏|12=draft}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=draft}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=draft}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅|12=draft}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=draft}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|12=draft}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=draft}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=draft|12=draft}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft|12=draft}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft|12=draft}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kasai City|ja|加西市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|12=draft|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft|12=draft}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=draft|12=draft}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft|12=draft}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft|12=draft}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji Rebellion|ja|平治の乱|12=draft|12=draft}} (1159), {{ill|Kenei|ja|建永|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=draft|12=draft}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|ja|池田輝政|12=draft|12=draft}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|ja|姫路藩|12=draft|12=draft}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft|12=draft}} ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Tōkei|ja|闘鶏|12=draft|12=draft}} and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyogo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=draft|12=draft}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=draft|12=draft}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=draft|12=draft}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine}}

Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyogo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hokujō Station|ja|北条町駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|ja|酒見寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Kasai City buildings]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine}}
