{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|qq=}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] |Foundation=1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyōgo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (731) associated with the deities of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|qq=}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] |Foundation=1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyōgo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (731) associated with the deities of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:加西市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|qq=}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] |Foundation=1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyōgo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (731) associated with the deities of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:加西市の建築物]] [[Category:加西市の歴史]] [[Category:Harima Province]]

In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:加西市の建築物]] [[Category:加西市の歴史]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kasai]] [[Category:加西市の歴史]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|qq=}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] |Foundation=1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyōgo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (731) associated with the deities of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kasai]] [[Category:History of Kasai, Hyogo]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (731) associated with the deities of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kasai]] [[Category:History of Kasai, Hyogo]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}}

Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Kasai City history]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kasai]] [[Category:History of Kasai, Hyogo]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] |Foundation=1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyōgo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (731) associated with the deities of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:住吉神社 (加西市)]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kasai]] [[Category:History of Kasai, Hyogo]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}}

Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kasai]] [[Category:History of Kasai, Hyogo]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sakami no Kami|ja|酒見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] |Foundation=1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property) |Map=Japan Hyōgo }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasai, Hyōgo|Kasai City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]], a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. ==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 3 (731) associated with the deities of [[:en:Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. In [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 17 (745), the adjacent [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] was founded by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the [[:en:Heiji rebellion|Heiji Rebellion]] (1159), [[:en:Ken'ei|Kenei]] 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under the patronage of [[:en:Ikeda Terumasa|Ikeda Terumasa]], who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, [[:en:Himeji Domain|Himeji Domain]] lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku. The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4 (1851). In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909. ==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] ==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as [[:en:Cockfighting|Tōkei]] and Dragon King Dance. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall ===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques) ===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine''' ==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|qq=}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō. Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region. ==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture ==Access== * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Hōjōmachi Station|Hokujō Station]] on the Hokujō Line ==Nearby Landmarks== * [[:en:Sagami-ji|Sagami-ji]] (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|qq=}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304132957/http://www.geocities.jp/sekku_matsuri423/index.htm Web Site Banshū Hokujō Setsuku Festival (Archived)] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (加西市)|version=103188860|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine]] [[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kasai]] [[Category:History of Kasai, Hyogo]] [[Category:Harima Province]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai}}
