<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381861]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)|1=Hōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402179}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg |Map=Japan Hyōgo |prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.935306|N|134.828556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]]
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}.
The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji Rebellion}} (1159), {{ill|Ken'ei|en|Ken'ei|WD=Q1194153|lt=Kenei}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.

===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381861]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)|1=Hōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402179}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg |Map=Japan Hyōgo |prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.935306|N|134.828556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]]
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}.
The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji Rebellion}} (1159), {{ill|Ken'ei|en|Ken'ei|WD=Q1194153|lt=Kenei}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}.

It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381861]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)|1=Hōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402179}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg |Map=Japan Hyōgo |prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.935306|N|134.828556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]]
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}.
The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji Rebellion}} (1159), {{ill|Ken'ei|en|Ken'ei|WD=Q1194153|lt=Kenei}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja, Kasai]][[ja:住吉神社 (加西市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)|1=Hōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402179}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg |Map=Japan Hyōgo |prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.935306|N|134.828556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]]
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}.
The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji Rebellion}} (1159), {{ill|Ken'ei|en|Ken'ei|WD=Q1194153|lt=Kenei}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)|1=Hōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402179}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg |Map=Japan Hyōgo |prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.935306|N|134.828556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]]
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}.
The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji Rebellion}} (1159), {{ill|Ken'ei|en|Ken'ei|WD=Q1194153|lt=Kenei}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Sumiyoshi Shrine |location={{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja|北条町北条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokujō Town Hokujō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北条町 (兵庫県)|1=Hōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402179}} 1318 |Latitude_deg=34 |Latitude_min=56 |Latitude_sec=7.1 |N/S=N |Longitude_deg=134 |Longitude_min=49 |Longitude_sec=42.8 |E/W=E |MapCode=JP |deity={{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013}} |Foundation=1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717) |Rank=Former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Reisai=Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) - First Saturday and Sunday of April |MainBuilding=Sumiyoshi-zukuri |Othernames=Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja03s3200.jpg |Map=Japan Hyōgo |prefecture={{ill|Kasai|WD=Q851155}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.935306|N|134.828556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Kasai sumiyoshi-jinja01s3200.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Worship Hall (National Important Cultural Property)]]
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==History== The shrine was originally established in Kurokoma Village and relocated to its current site in the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (717). It is one of the nine shrines listed in the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|ja|住吉大社神代記|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles|qq=}}, a document from {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 3 (731) associated with the deities of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. Historically referred to as Sakami Daimyōjin or Sakami Shrine, the current name was adopted when it was designated a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 17 (745), the adjacent {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} was founded by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}.
The shrine has undergone numerous cycles of destruction and rebuilding, including during the {{ill|Heiji rebellion|en|Heiji rebellion|WD=Q944859|lt=Heiji Rebellion}} (1159), {{ill|Ken'ei|en|Ken'ei|WD=Q1194153|lt=Kenei}} 2 (1207), and the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天正|1=Tenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1145058}} (1573–1592). It was restored during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under the patronage of {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|WD=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa}}, who donated 30 koku annually. Subsequently, {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|WD=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain}} lord Honda Minonokami increased the shrine’s land holdings to 60 koku.
The current main buildings (National Important Cultural Properties) were reconstructed in {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4 (1851). In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 7 (1874), the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and elevated to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It absorbed Ōtoshi Shrine and Hachiman Shrine in 1909.
==deity== * Principal Deities: Sakami no Kami, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja|住吉四神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Four Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sokotutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Empress Jingū), {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}
==Precincts== * '''Middle Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''East Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''West Main Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851, renovated in 1916. * '''Worship Hall''' (National Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808 (Bunka 5). * '''Heiden''' (Kasai City Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1916. * '''White Beard Shrine''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1851. * '''Two Inari Shrines''' * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in 1808. * '''Treasury Hall''' * '''Chokushi Mound''' - Used for rituals such as {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tōkei}} and Dragon King Dance.
==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== * '''Four Structures''' (Designated December 12, 2022) ** East Main Hall ** Middle Main Hall ** West Main Hall ** Worship Hall
===Hyōgo Prefecture Cultural Properties=== * '''White Beard Shrine''' (with associated ridge plaque) * '''Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion)''' (with associated plaques)
===Kasai City Cultural Properties=== * '''Heiden''' * '''Awashima Shrine'''
==Spring Grand Festival (Hokujō Setsuku Festival) == Held annually on the first Saturday and Sunday of April, this festival is one of the three major festivals of {{ill|Banshū|ja|播州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 播磨国|1=Harima Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047523}}. Similar to the {{ill|Kakogawa matsuri|ja|播州の秋祭り|fr|Kakogawa matsuri|lt=Autumn Festival of Banshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa matsuri|1=Banshū Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498705}}, it features "koshiro-yatai" (decorative floats) or drum platforms known locally as "kesho-yatai." A total of 15 floats are mobilized from the East and West groups of Hokujō.
Participants wear traditional kimono with nagajuban undergarments, an attire unique to the region.
==location== 1318 Hokujō Town, Kasai City, Hyōgo Prefecture
==Access== * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Hōjōmachi Station|en|Hōjōmachi Station|WD=Q5965208|lt=Hokujō Station}} on the Hokujō Line
==Nearby Landmarks== * {{ill|Sagami-ji|en|Sagami-ji|WD=Q3137229|lt=Sagami-ji}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Rakan Temple|ja|羅漢寺 (加西市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rakan Temple|1=Rakan-ji Temple, Kasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11608594}} - Formerly a branch of Sakami Temple. * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} - Adjacent to Kasaya float storehouse. * {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrines|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine|ja|大年神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大歳神社}} - Pilgrimage station with a float storehouse. * Aeon Mall Kasai Hokujō * Kasai City Hokujō Elementary School

'''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasai, Hyōgo|en|Kasai, Hyōgo|WD=Q851155|lt=Kasai City|1=Kasai, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. Also known as Sakami Daimyōjin, Sumiyoshi Sakami Shrine, or Sumiyoshi Sakami Jinja, it is the third shrine of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}, a former {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
